title: elymus melantherus
intro: Elymus melantherus, also known as Black-grass or Wild Rye, is a species of grass native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Elymus melantherus include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
